dsHPC 0.0.0.9000

    • Python Integration with dsHPC

dsHPC Package Integration Plan

Source: INTEGRATION_PLAN.md

Background

The dsHPC package is designed to be used exclusively within the DataSHIELD environment to submit jobs to Slurm. However, the current codebase has two parallel sets of functions:

  1. Regular functions (dsHPC.init, dsHPC.submit, etc.)
  2. DataSHIELD-specific functions (dsHPC.ds.init, dsHPC.ds.submit, etc.)

This duplication creates confusion and maintenance overhead. This document outlines a plan to streamline the codebase.

Design Decisions

  1. Unified Interface: The package should have a single set of functions that always operate in the DataSHIELD context.
  2. Direct Slurm Integration: All job submissions should target Slurm (with local execution fallback only if unavailable).
  3. Simplified Code Structure: Remove redundant DS-specific wrappers and integrate any DataSHIELD-specific logic into the main functions.

Implementation Plan

1. Function Changes

Main Functions (in R/dsHPC.R)

  • Modify dsHPC.init() to:
    • Check if running in DataSHIELD environment (using is_datashield_env())
    • If not, issue a warning that the package is designed for DataSHIELD
    • Continue with initialization but note that some features may not work outside DataSHIELD
  • Update all job submission functions (dsHPC.submit(), etc.) to:
    • Check if running in DataSHIELD environment
    • Incorporate any DataSHIELD-specific logic currently in the wrapper functions
    • Proceed with submission to Slurm (or local execution if not available)

DataSHIELD Wrapper Functions (in R/dsHPC_methods.R)

  • Keep these functions temporarily for backward compatibility
  • Modify them to:
    • Issue a deprecation warning suggesting the use of their non-DS counterparts
    • Call the regular functions without additional logic
  • Document that these will be removed in a future version

2. Documentation Updates

  • Update all function documentation to clarify they are designed to run within DataSHIELD
  • Remove examples that suggest the functions can be used outside DataSHIELD
  • Add notes about Slurm integration being the primary mode of operation

3. Tests

  • Update tests to reflect the new design
  • Remove tests that test DS-specific functions independently
  • Ensure tests verify that functions work correctly within a simulated DataSHIELD environment

4. Code to Update

The following files need modifications:

  • R/dsHPC.R: Update main functions
  • R/dsHPC_methods.R: Mark DS functions as deprecated
  • R/utils.R: Enhance is_datashield_env() if needed
  • tests/testthat/: Update tests

5. Documentation to Update

  • DESCRIPTION: Clarify the package is for DataSHIELD
  • README.md: Update examples and explanations
  • vignettes/: Ensure examples reflect the DataSHIELD-centric approach
  • man/: Update Roxygen documentation for all functions

Timeline

  1. Phase 1: Update documentation and main functions
  2. Phase 2: Deprecate DS-specific functions
  3. Phase 3: Update tests
  4. Phase 4: In a future version, completely remove the deprecated functions

Backward Compatibility

To maintain backward compatibility during the transition:

  1. Keep DS-specific functions but mark them as deprecated
  2. Ensure main functions still work with existing code
  3. Provide migration guidance in the documentation

Conclusion

This plan will streamline the dsHPC package to match its intended use case: providing HPC functionality within the DataSHIELD environment. The changes will reduce code duplication, clarify the package’s purpose, and ensure all future development follows a consistent approach.

On this page
